title: Mediterranean Veggie & Feta Egg Casserole
description: A fresh, herby egg casserole with zucchini, cherry tomatoes, spinach, and feta. Works as a make-ahead breakfast or light dinner. Optional sausage for non-vegetarian version.

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9x13 baking dish.
If using sausage, cook it in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart, until browned. Set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté zucchini until tender, about 5 minutes. Add spinach and stir until wilted. Remove from heat.
Whisk together eggs, milk, oregano, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Spread sausage (if using) and sautéed vegetables in the baking dish. Scatter cherry tomatoes and feta over the top. Pour egg mixture evenly over everything.
Bake 35–40 minutes until the center is set and the top is lightly golden. Rest 5 minutes before slicing.

notes:
Without sausage this is a clean, vegetarian meal. With sausage it's no longer healthy -- choose based on your goals.

Make-ahead: assemble the night before, cover and refrigerate, bake in the morning.

Freezer-friendly: bake, cool completely, cut into squares, and freeze up to 2 months. Reheat individual squares in the microwave or oven.

Veggie rule: always pre-cook watery vegetables like zucchini and mushrooms before adding -- skipping this step leads to a soggy casserole.
